title: Set Harth
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Dark Jedi |image=[[File:DarthSmug-UA.jpg]] |name=Set Harth |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|White]]/silver |feathers= |eyes=Blue |skin=Milky white |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] |masters=*[[Aru-Wen]] {{C|[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]}}''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' *[[Obba]] {{C|Jedi Master}} |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Not much different from what you do now. You've managed to stay one step ahead of the Jedi all these years; how much harder can it be to stay one step ahead of the Sith at the same time?|Set Harth, pondering the dilemma he was put into|}} '''Set Harth''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] active during the decades following the [[Ruusan Reformations/Legends|Ruusan Reformations]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Once a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], his [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Aru-Wen]] was slain during the waning days of the [[Ruusan campaign]] in the [[New Sith Wars]]. After Aru-Wen's [[Death/Legends|death]] during the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan|final battle of the war]] on [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]], Harth was trained by Master [[Obba]] of the [[Council of First Knowledge/Legends|Council of First Knowledge]]. Obba's obsession in the research of [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifacts]] led to Harth's fascination with the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], and Harth fell to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. Harth then abandoned the Order and moved to [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]], where he set up a [[Set Harth's mansion|mansion]], as well as on other [[Planet/Legends|planets]] under his many disguises, to store all the [[Dark side artifact/Legends|dark side artifacts]] he could find. In [[980 BBY]], Harth recovered several artifacts that had been [[Mining/Legends|excavated]] on [[Doan]]. However, he was [[Mission to Doan|tracked down]] and [[Duel in Set Harth's mansion|bested]] by [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Zannah/Legends|Darth Zannah]], who forced him to ally with her while she hunted down her [[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]], [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]]. Together, Harth and Zannah found out that Bane had been [[Skirmish in Darth Bane's mansion|captured]] and taken to the [[Stone Prison]] in Doan. Zannah went inside to [[Duel on Doan|confront]] her master, leaving Harth to guard their ship. However, Harth entered the [[Prison/Legends|prison]] and found the [[Holocron of Heresies|holocron]] of [[Darth Andeddu]], which had been taken from Bane following the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord's]] capture. Harth then fled the prison, but not before briefly encountering Zannah apprentice, [[Darth Cognus]], and began studying the method of [[transfer essence]] under the holocron's [[Gatekeeper/Legends|gatekeeper]]. Combining this power with clone bodies, Harth was able to live at least until the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. ==Biography== ===Life as a Jedi=== {{Quote|As a Padawan he lost his Master to the thought bomb on Ruusan. I took him under my wing, and eventually I recommended him to the other members on the Council of First Knowledge. Like Medd, he became one of our agents, scouring the galaxy for dark side artifacts and lore.|Jedi Master Obba|}} A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Human/Legends|Human]], Set Harth was inducted into the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] under the tutelage of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Aru-Wen]] at the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]]. When the [[Battlemaster/Legends|Temple battlemaster]], [[Jedi Lord]] [[Hoth (Jedi Lord)|Hoth]], raised the [[Army of Light/Legends|Army of Light]] from among the holdings of the Jedi Lords spread across the galaxy, Harth's master departed Coruscant to join in the fighting. Harth joined him briefly, succumbing to his anger while battling a warrior of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Lord]] [[Skere Kaan|Kaan's]] [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]]. Due to Harth's violent tendencies, Aru-Wen ordered his Padawan to return to the Coruscant Temple while he traveled to [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]] to join the Army of Light for the final showdown with Kaan. During the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan]], Kaan used a [[thought bomb]] on the Army of Light, killing exactly 100 members of it who were selected to go down with Lord Hoth, including Aru-Wen. Following his Master's death on Ruusan, Harth came under the tutelage of Master [[Obba]]. Working with the [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]] Jedi, Harth began working with the [[Council of First Knowledge/Legends|Council of First Knowledge]] in their endeavors to locate and confiscate all [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifacts]] scattered across the galaxy. As he was assigned more and more tasks, Harth began to horde the artifacts he found, until eventually he had enough money to leave the Order and learn the ways of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]. ===A fallen Jedi=== {{Quote|He sounds like a dangerous man.''"
"''Now that the Sith are extinct, Set Harth may be the most dangerous individual in the galaxy.|Princess Serra, and Master Obba|}} [[File:Bal_SerinusversusSet_Harth.jpg|thumb|left|165px|Set Harth (left) and Bal Serinus duel on Nar Shaddaa.]] Harth heard rumors of a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] artifact that had survived the catastrophe on Ruusan. He tracked the artifact to an abandoned Sith armory on the Ruusan battlefields, where he battled a dark side warrior for possession of the artifact. Harth defeated the warrior and gained possession of the artifact, which further corrupted him. Harth set up a [[Set Harth's mansion|mansion]] on [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]] and began searching for other Sith artifacts. One of Harth's searches led him to the palace of a [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Slavery/Legends|slaver]], where he encountered another [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]], [[Bal Serinus]]. The two dueled each other to exhaustion. Before the two dark siders could finish their duel, they were both caught by the Hutt slaver. While they were imprisoned in separate cells, Harth and Serinus [[Telepathy/Legends|communicated]] through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Harth promised himself to protect Serinus from harm until she suddenly rebuffed his telepathic contact, in light of which Harth took a vow of vengeance. ===Evolution of a hedonist and Sith Lord=== {{Quote|If you're going to kill somebody so you can steal the credits, at least go after an easy target.|Harth to Quano after the Rodian's failed assassination attempt|}} In [[980 BBY]], Harth traveled to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Doan]] to recover several recently [[Mining/Legends|excavated]] [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifacts]]. He forced a [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Bartender/Legends|bartender]], [[Quano]], to take him to the miner who had dug up the artifacts, [[Draado]]. As Draado attempted to rally the miners to go to war with the [[Doan Royal House]] in retaliation of the recent [[Massacre on Doan|assassination]] of their leader, [[Gelba]], Harth ambushed them. The other miners were quickly killed, but Draado survived for a short time due to the help of the artifacts he wore. However, Harth managed to [[Telekinesis/Legends#Choking powers|Force choke]] the miner and kill him before taking the artifacts from him and returning to Nal Hutta. [[File:SetHarthDarkJedi.jpg|thumb|right|120px|Set Harth, Dark Jedi and Sith apprentice]] After Harth returned home, [[Darth Zannah/Legends|Darth Zannah]], the [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]] of [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]], [[Mission to Doan|tracked]] Harth to Nal Hutta and, after a [[Duel in Set Harth's mansion|brief duel]] in [[Set Harth's mansion|Harth's estate]], offered him the opportunity to become her own apprentice. Harth realized that if he refused, she would kill him, and accepted. The two traveled to [[Darth Bane's mansion|Bane's home]] on [[Ciutric IV]], where Zannah and Bane had been living, only to discover that Bane had been [[Skirmish in Darth Bane's mansion|captured]] by the [[Doan Royal Guard|Royal Guard]] of the [[Doan Royal House]]. Unbeknownst to Zannah and Harth, [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Serra]] of Doan, who had ordered the attack, was actually [[Caleb]]'s daughter. She sought revenge on Bane, whom she mistakenly believed had [[Confrontation on Ambria|killed]] her father. Zannah and Harth returned to Doan to rescue Bane from the [[Stone Prison]], but he escaped from his cell with the help of Serra's bodyguard [[Lucia (soldier)|Lucia]], who had fought under Bane as a member of the [[Gloom Walkers]]. Zannah ordered Harth to watch her vessel, the [[Victory (Theta-class T-1 vessel)|''Victory'']], before entering the [[Prison/Legends|prison]], where she located Bane and challenged him to a [[Duel on Doan|duel]], hoping to defeat him and become the next [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]. However, Harth disobeyed Zannah and entered the prison, where he found and stole the [[Holocron of Heresies]], which had been taken from Bane after Bane's capture. As Harth fled, he decided to steal a starship, the [[Stalker (shuttle)|''Stalker'']]. However, before he could escape, the starship's owner, [[Darth Cognus|the Huntress]]—the [[Iktotchi/Legends|Iktotchi]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] who had captured Bane—arrived and challenged him. After a [[Skirmish in the Stone Prison hangar|fight]] in the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] in which the Huntress was able to gain the upper hand, but before she could kill him, Serra— who was hoping to kill the two dueling Sith Lords—set the Prison to self destruct. Harth then disabled two of the four ships in the hangar with his lightsaber and proposed to the Huntress that either they both should go free in their own ships or he would destroy both ships and they would be killed in the collapsing prison together. The Huntress accepted, allowing him to flee in his starship, taking the holocron with him.Soon after he began lessons from the holocrons gatekeeper on [https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Dark_side_of_the_Force/Legends the dark side of the force], and the technique of [https://starwars.fandom.com/wiki/Transfer_essence essence transfer]. ===Legendary explorer=== [[File:Set_Harth.jpg|thumb|left|Set Harth]] Fearing Zannah would find him on Nal Hutta if she survived the Stone Prison's destruction, Harth knew better than to go back to his usual location. Since he had other bases under false identities, there was no need for him to run aimlessly across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Having sole possession of [[Darth Andeddu]]'s holocron, Harth was free to access the device and ply its storage banks for hidden knowledge. Pledging himself to the gatekeeper of the holocron, Harth agreed to train under Andeddu in order to eventually earn the ability to [[Transfer essence|transfer his consciousness]] into other bodies. After some time, Harth conquered the ability and eventually contacted [[Cloning/Legends|cloning specialists]] who were able to reproduce his original body. For over one thousand years, Harth continued to clone his original body over and over, usually discarding his clone bodies at age 30. Over the centuries, Harth gathered many collections of Sith artifacts scattered in caches across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], as well as numerous starships and vast resources. Harth became a legendary explorer, his continued existence suspected by but unproven to the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] as he continued to scour the galaxy for dark side artifacts.''[[Ultimate Adversaries]]'' ===Legacy=== Because of Harth's desertion from the [[Order of the Sith Lords]], Zannah realized that taking him in as an apprentice had been a mistake. However, after she [[Duel on Ambria (980 BBY)|defeated]] Bane on [[Ambria/Legends|Ambria]], Zannah took on a more willing apprentice: the Huntress, who had taken the name of Darth Cognus before Zannah became the Dark Lord. While in Harth's possession for some time, Andeddu's holocron was discovered on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] in [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]] by Sith Lord [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Dooku/Legends|Tyranus]].[[Republic 63|''Republic'' 63]] It is unknown how he was parted from this relic, but it could not have been due to his final death as Harth was known to have lived past the Clone Wars. ==Personality and traits== During the war with the Brotherhood of Darkness, the Jedi High Council's strict recruiting policies were loosened, allowing less than ideal candidates to receive training. Set Harth was one of these exceptions and, though Harth was allowed into the Jedi Order his master, Aru-Wen, felt that his violent tendencies indicated a dangerous path and left him on Coruscant. Harth continued to take these paths because of his arrogance and allegiance to himself. Even when faced with a situation as dangerous as Darth Zannah and the legacy of the Sith, he pulled off a betrayal that made him a legend. ==Powers and abilities== {{Quote|Now that the Sith are extinct, Set Harth may be the most dangerous individual in the galaxy.|Jedi Master Obba|}} Set Harth had considerable skill with [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinesis]], throwing objects directly at his opponents to dislodge them. When he fell to the dark side, he learned to utilize Force Choke on his enemies, as well as Force Grip and [[Force drain/Legends|Force drain]]. He was also talented in [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathy]], [[Battlemind]], [[Force sight]], and the [[Mind trick/Legends|mind trick]], which he perfected into Force fear as a Dark Jedi. Before turning his back on Darth Zannah, he realized his full potential when he could expand his awareness, due to some early part of his training under Obba. He later learned how to transfer his essence into other beings, which he used to extend his life indefinitely and thus lived for more than a millennium after he acquired this power. This extended to his knowledge of [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]]. Harth was a practitioner of his own stylized version of [[Form IV/Legends|Ataru]], which he displayed in his duel with Darth Zannah. However, the form had exhausted him greatly, giving Zannah an advantage to defeat him. He was also quite proficient with throwing his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], a move that nearly killed Zannah in their first confrontation and also the Huntress on Doan; both women only survived due to their precognitive abilities. Harth was spared by Zannah for his skill and because of her desperate need of an apprentice. During his life as a Dark Jedi, while carrying a lightsaber, Harth also equipped himself with two [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] to hide his affinity to the Force: a [[GSI-24D disruptor pistol]] and a conventional blaster. He was quite skilled with the blasters, easily taking down many miners in his skirmish with them on Doan. ==Behind the scenes== Set Harth appeared in ''[[Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil]]'', the final novel in the [[Star Wars: Darth Bane Trilogy|''Darth Bane Trilogy'']], written by [[Drew Karpyshyn]]. ==Appearances== *''[[Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil]]'' {{Ab|Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil (audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Set Harth}} *''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Core Rulebook]]'' {{1stm}} *''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' *''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Revised Core Rulebook]]'' *''[[Ultimate Adversaries]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{Topps|set=2011 Topps Star Wars Galaxy Series 6|cardname=NNO37 - Jason Kieth Phillips}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Set Harth |es=Set Harth |pl=Set Harth |pt=Set Harth }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Harth, Set}} [[Category:Fallen Jedi]] [[Category:Force-sensitive clones]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Impersonators]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Jedi Guardians]] [[Category:Jedi Purge survivors]] [[Category:Jedi trainees of the Jedi Order]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Pilots]] [[Category:Space explorers]] [[Category:Treasure keepers]]
